title: Rama’s Exile
summary: Rama accepts 14 years of exile to honor his father’s promise.
ethical_principle: Duty and honor should be upheld even at personal cost.

title: Bharata’s Refusal of Throne
summary: Bharata refuses to rule Ayodhya in Rama’s absence.
ethical_principle: Power should not be taken unjustly.

title: Sita’s Loyalty
summary: Sita chooses to accompany Rama into exile.

title: Lakshmana’s Service
summary: Lakshmana serves Rama selflessly during exile.

title: Ravana’s Arrogance
summary: Ravana ignores wise advice due to ego.

title: Hanuman’s Devotion
summary: Hanuman risks his life to find Sita.
ethical_principle: Courage fueled by devotion achieves the impossible.

title: Vibhishana’s Defection
summary: Vibhishana leaves Ravana to support righteousness.
ethical_principle: Stand with truth even against family.

title: The Golden Deer
summary: Sita desires the magical deer, leading to her abduction.

title: Jatayu’s Sacrifice
summary: Jatayu fights Ravana to save Sita.
ethical_principle: Bravery in defense of others is noble.

title: Sugriva’s Promise
summary: Sugriva initially delays helping Rama after gaining power.

title: Vali’s Death
summary: Rama kills Vali strategically from concealment.
ethical_principle: Ethical decisions in war are complex.

title: Bridge to Lanka
summary: Vanara army builds a bridge to Lanka.

title: Agni Pariksha
summary: Sita undergoes trial by fire to prove purity.
ethical_principle: Societal expectations can challenge personal dignity.

title: Rama’s Rule (Rama Rajya)
summary: Rama rules with fairness and justice.
ethical_principle: Ideal leadership balances compassion and law.

title: Shabari’s Devotion
summary: Shabari waits patiently for Rama’s arrival.
